title: Rich Midnight Middle-Eastern Dessert Pudding
description: A sweet and creamy Middle-Eastern dessert pudding featuring a hint of mustardy flavor, blue food colouring, and a sugar-free friendly option. Serve warm or chilled, garnished with toasted pistachios and a drizzle of honey.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, whisk together the almond milk, sugar-free sweetener, blue food colouring, and ground mustard seeds. Heat over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a simmer.
Whisk in the coconut cream, lemon juice, vanilla extract, and salt. Continue to cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the mixture thickens slightly.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let cool slightly. Stir in the pistachio meal, honey, and ghee, if using.
Pour the pudding mixture into 6-8 small ramekins or a large donabe. Cover with plastic wrap, pressing the wrap directly onto the surface of the pudding to prevent a skin from forming.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until chilled and set. Serve warm or chilled, garnished with toasted pistachios and a drizzle of honey.
If using a donabe, cook over low heat for 10-15 minutes, or until the pudding is heated through and slightly puffed.
Note: If using a sugar-free sweetener, adjust the amount to taste.
Note: If using a donabe, be careful not to overcook the pudding, as it can quickly become too thick and sticky.
Note: If you prefer a creamier pudding, you can add an additional 1/4 cup of coconut cream or half-and-half.
Note: You can also flavor the pudding with a pinch of ground cinnamon or cardamom for added depth and warmth.
